characters.name: Albert Schiffer
characters.description: Lieutenat Albert Schifferwas a colleague ofLieutenant Columboat the Los Angeles Police Department. In October 1994 he responded to the fatal shooting of mob bookieBruno RomanoatGraham McVeigh's house. He was played byJustin Lord.

characters.name: Andy Parma
characters.description: Andy ParmaisColumbo's nephew. His father was Columbo's brother, and tragically both of Andy's parents died in an airplane crash when Andy was young. After this he began to look up to his uncle like a father, and followed in his footsteps by joining the Los Angeles Police Department, eventually making Detective. He became good friends with fellow officersRobert GoodmanandDennis Mulrooney. In 1992, Andy married the love of his life, aspiring supermodelMelissa Alexandra Hayes, but disaster struck when Melissa was abducted from their hotel bridal suite by deranged stalkerRudy Strassa. Andy, his friends and veteran uncle were soon on the case, and thankfully managed to rescue Melissa before she could be killed. Andy appeared inNo Time to Die, played by soap opera starThomas Calabro. Andy is the only member of Columbo's family to ever appear on screen in the series.

characters.name: Arthur Brown
characters.description: Detective Sergeant Arthur Brownworked in the Los Angeles Police Department and was a friend and colleague ofLieutenant Columbo. Together they investigated the deaths ofJ.J. DillingerandEugene E. Ehrbachin 1994, which resulted in them cracking the case of a missing 4 million dollars. Brown appeared inUndercover, played byHarrison Page. The episode is based on the novelJigsawbyEd McBain, an entry in his87th Precinctseries. While most character names were changed for the adaptation, Sergeant Brown is the only87th Precinctcharacter to have featured onColumbo. The character appeared in several other novels, includingSo Long as You Both Shall Live, which was filmed asNo Time to Die.

characters.name: Barney
characters.description: Barneywas the proprietor ofBarney's Beanery, a famous chili restaurant in West Hollywood. The beanery was a favourite haunt ofLieutenant Columbo, a notorious chili hound, and he dined there on a number of occasions. Barney was played byColumboregularJohn Finneganand appeared in three episodes;It's All in the Gamein 1993,Strange Bedfellowsin 1995 andA Trace of Murderin 1997. Among Finnegan's many previous appearances on the show was asFredinUneasy Lies the Crown, a waiter who serves Columbo his latest chili dish. Maybe he and Barney were brothers.

characters.name: Baxter
characters.description: Baxterwas employed as a security guard at the Van Wick residence in Los Angeles byHarold Van Wickin 1975. InPlaybackhe was played byHerbert Jefferson Jr..

characters.name: Captain Ortega
characters.description: Captain Ortegawas an L.A.P.D. Captain of Detectives assigned to theSuarian task forceduring a 1975 diplomatic visit to California by KingAhmed Kamal. He appeared inA Case of Immunity, played byJay Varela.

characters.name: Carl
characters.description: Carlwas the construction foreman atElliot Markham's construction site at1801 Century Park Eastin 1972. He was present when Markham trickedLieutenant Columbointo digging up the site, hoping to uncoverBeau Williamson's body. InBlueprint for Murder, Carl was played by recurring bit playerJohn Finneganin his first appearance on the show.

characters.name: Charles
characters.description: Charleswas a manservant in the employ of the Chadwick family, working underBryce Chadwickand his sisterBeth. After Beth murdered Bryce and was arrested byLieutenant Columbo, it is unknown what became of Charles or the other staff. InLady in Waiting, Charles is played byJoel Fluellen.

characters.name: Dennis Mulrooney
characters.description: Detective Dennis Mulrooneyis an L.A.P.D. detective and colleague to DetectiveAndy Parmaand SergeantRobert Goodman. A close friend of Andy's, Mulrooney was invited to his wedding to modelMelissa Alexandra Hayes. When Melissa was subsequently abducted from the bridal suite by deranged stalkerRudy Strassa, Mulrooney worked alongside Andy, Goodman and Andy's uncle, veteran homicide detectiveLieutenant Columbo, to rescue her. Detective Mulrooney appeared in the 1992Season 10episodeNo Time to Die, played byDoug Savant.

characters.name: Detective Braverman
characters.description: Detective Bravermanwas one of two L.A.P.D. homicide detectives who investigated the scene ofFreddy Brower's death on October 31, 1991. He andDetective Lancerwere met at Freddy's apartment byLieutenant Columbo, where they explained that it appeared that Freddy has slipped and fallen while taking a bath, hit his head on the way down and subsequently drowned. But a few small details bothered Columbo, who eventually uncovered a murder plot concocted by Freddy's soon-to-be-ex-wifeNancyand his dastardly uncleLeon Lamarr. Detective Braverman made a brief appearance inDeath Hits the Jackpot, played byDaniel Trent.

characters.name: Detective Lancer
characters.description: Detective Lancerwas one of two L.A.P.D. homicide detectives who investigated the scene ofFreddy Brower's death on October 31, 1991. He andDetective Bravermanwere met at Freddy's apartment byLieutenant Columbo, where they explained that it appeared that Freddy has slipped and fallen while taking a bath, hit his head on the way down and subsequently drowned. But a few small details bothered Columbo, who eventually uncovered a murder plot concocted by Freddy's soon-to-be-ex-wifeNancyand his dastardly uncleLeon Lamarr. Detective Lancer made a brief appearance inDeath Hits the Jackpot, played byRobert Alan Browne.

characters.name: Francine
characters.description: Francine was the owner of Grant Galleries inPlayback. Francine was a friend of Harold Van Wick who invited he and his wife, Elizabeth, to a party at her art gallery, Grant Galleries, to see some new pieces she had attained. Elizabeth didn't wish to attend, allowing Harold the opportunity to use the party in combination with the playback of a tape recording to fashion himself an alibi for the time that he murdered his mother in-law, Margaret Meadis. Later, Francine is visited by Lieutenant Columbo, who is investigating the Meadis murder case. The lieutenant is baffled by the various art pieces she has in the gallery, even managing to mistake the ventilation cover for a piece of modern art. Francine allows Columbo to have Harold's invitation to the party, which ends up being the crucial piece of evidence that damns Mr Van Wick beyond a shadow of a doubt to having been the murderer due to it being visible in the recording of Margaret being shot. Francine was played by Patricia Barry.

characters.name: Fred (Uneasy Lies The Crown)
characters.description: FredservesLieutenant Columbohis chili inUneasy Lies the Crown. He seems to know Columbo well, and has probably been serving him chili for a while. So dedicated was he to the job that he kept right on working even when he had a cold... Ew. Fred the waiter was played by an uncreditedJohn Finnegan, who would go on to playa completelydifferentcharacterwho serves Columbo chili inIt's All in the Game,Strange BedfellowsandA Trace of Murder.

characters.name: George
characters.description: Georgewas the Chief Medical Examiner for the Los Angeles Police Department during the early 1990s. He was often consulted on homicide cases byLieutenant Columbo. He first appeared inUneasy Lies the Crown, then returned inColumbo Goes to CollegeandCaution: Murder Can Be Hazardous to Your Health. His final appearance was inColumbo and the Murder of a Rock Star. He was portrayed bySteven Gilborn.

characters.name: George Kramer
characters.description: Sergeant George Krameris an L.A.P.D. homicide detective who has worked withLieutenant Columboon a number of occasions. Played byBruce Kirby, Sergeant Kramer is the most frequently recurring character onColumbo, having appeared in six episodes;By Dawn's Early Light,A Deadly State of Mind,Identity Crisis,Last Salute to the Commodore,Columbo Cries WolfandAgenda for Murder. Four of these episodes were starred in and/or directed byPatrick McGoohanwho evidently liked working with Kirby. Kirby additionally played a lab attendant namedDouginLovely But Lethal, a TV repairman inMake Me a Perfect Murderand, somewhat bizarrely, a completely different police sergeant namedPhil BrindleinStrange Bedfellows.

characters.name: Harry Stone
characters.description: Harry StonewasNelson Hayward's campaign manager during his 1973 run to become a United States Senator. Together, they cooked up a scheme to generate publicity and sympathy votes for Hayward, by sending Hayward "threatening" letters that they had in fact written themselves. However what Harry didn't know was that Hayward was setting Harry up to be an accomplice to his own death; Harry had found out Nelson was having an affair with his wife's secretary Linda Johnson. Realizing the relationship was a public relations nightmare ready to happen, Harry demanded that Nelson break off the relationship at once. Nelson seemingly agreed, arranging fir Harry to wear his clothes and lure his police escort on a wild chase so he could speak to Linda in person and precent her from doing anything rash. Once the ruse went off, he would later shoot Harry to death at his beach house, making it look like a case of mistaken identity (the police assuming an assassin had wrongfully killed Harry while trying to kill Hayward). Hayward didn't count onLieutenant Columbobeing on the case however and his entire scheme was eventually toppled on election night.

characters.name: Hugh Creighton
characters.description: Hugh Creightonwas the best murder defense lawyer in the country. His high-priced reputation preceded him; he never lost a case in his entire career. This was, of course, because he wasn't afraid to bribe witnesses. And since his longtime loverMarcy Edwardsknew this, she had to die, especially if she was going to cheat on him with someone else! Creighton enlisted the help of his assistantTrish Fairbanksin concocting the perfect alibi, and in return he made her a full partner in his firm. However Creighton's house of cards finally came crashing down when matched with the one opponent he could not bribe;Lieutenant Columbo. Hugh Creighton appeared in the 1991 episodeColumbo and the Murder of a Rock Star, portrayed by American actorDabney Coleman. Coleman was close real-life friend ofPeter Falkand had previously appeared asSergeant Murrayin 1972'sDouble Shock. He was nominated for an Emmy for his performance as Creighton.

characters.name: Jack Stroller
characters.description: Detective Jack Strollerwas an L.A.P.D. homicide detective who worked withLieutenant Columboon the$30 million lottery Halloween murder casein early November 1991. He was played byWarren BerlingerinDeath Hits the Jackpot.

characters.name: Janice Caldwell
characters.description: Janice Caldwellwas the wife ofHugh Caldwelluntil one night in 1974 when, during a furious argument, he strangled her to death. She appeared inA Friend in Deedas the first of two murder victims, the second beingMargaret Halperin. The actress playing Janice, who only appears as a lifeless corpse in the episode's opening moments, was uncredited and remains unidentified.

characters.name: Jim Ferris
characters.description: Jim Ferriswas one half of a mystery-writing duo withKen Franklinuntil 1971, when Ferris, who had done all of the actual writing, wanting to break it off to go solo. Franklin, who by this time had become accustomed to an expensive lifestyle, realized that this would leave him high-and-dry so he decided to murder Ferris. He did so by luring Ferris to a secluded cabin under the premise of letting him mull the decision over before shooting him to death. However, Franklin was eventually caught out and arrested byLieutenant Columbo. In the 1971season 1episodeMurder by the Book, Jim Ferris was portrayed by American actorMartin Milner.

characters.name: Joanna Ferris
characters.description: Joanna Ferris was the wife of Jim Ferris inMurder By The Book. Joanna was called by Jim one evening to say that he would be late home because he wanted to work late finishing writing the chapter he was busy authoring. This was a lie, as Jim was in actuality going to be late due to having been invited by Ken Franklin to spend one final day together at his new lake house before the two split apart. However, before the call could end, a gunshot was heard on the other end. Understandably unsettled by this, Joanna called the police and had them investigate Jim's office for clues of what may have happened. After a while, Joanna was taken aside by Lieutenant Columbo, who attempted to comfort her and put her at ease by making her an omelette whilst he questioned her. Ken Franklin later arrived and further comforted Joanna. Unbeknownst to her, Ken was responsible for Jim's death, having been the one who shot him. Joanna was distraught when Jim's body turned up, proving that he was dead. Later, Columbo requested her permission to search Jim's office for evidence of Ken's guilt after she mentioned to him that Jim wrote down all his story ideas. This was so that he could find a note that Jim had made to himself of an idea for the plot of a novel which was the same way that he himself had been murdered. Joanna Ferris was played by Rosemary Forsyth.

characters.name: Ken Franklin
characters.description: Ken Franklinwas one half of a mystery-writing duo withJim Ferrisuntil 1971, when Ferris, who had done all of the actual writing, wanting to break it off to go solo. Franklin, who by this time had become accustomed to an expensive lifestyle, realized that this would leave him high-and-dry so he decided to murder Ferris. He was later forced to murder general store ownerLily La Sankawhen she tried to blackmail him with her own witness testimony. In the end, however, Franklin was caught out and arrested byLieutenant Columbo. In the 1971season 1episodeMurder by the Book, Ken Franklin was portrayed by American actorJack Cassidy, who later play the villain two otherColumboepisodes;Publish or PerishandNow You See Him.

characters.name: Lieutenant Columbo
characters.description: Lieutenant ColumboCurrent1968  Lieutenant Franklin "Frank" Columbois a police detective in theLos Angeles Police Department. Frank Columbo was born to Italian-American parents in New York City. Growing up, his hero was Joe DiMaggio. He also liked gangster pictures. Ironically, when growing up, he was not a model child, as he ran with a crowd of boys that broke street lamps and played pranks. Columbo himself jokes that he joined the police force in a part to make up for these pranks. He has a wife whose first name is never mentioned but is referred to as 'Mrs. Columbo'. A picture of her is shown in the episode:Rest in Peace, Mrs. Columbo, However, Columbo reveals later in that episode that it's actually a picture of his sister in law because his wife doesn't like to have pictures taken of her. Columbo also has a Basset Hound and in the episode, 'Murder A Self Portrait' Columbo says the name of the dog is 'Dog'. He states that he does not have any children inRest in Peace, Mrs. Columbobut this is almost certainly a lie as it was told to a woman who wanted to kill his wife. On the other hand, he mentions children to people he likes and trusts inAny Old Port In A Storm.This suggests that he does really have children, although number, gender and other details are not stated. The novels reveal more detail about Columbo's children: In The Grassy Knoll, he talks about 2 of his kids going to a certain college meaning he has at least 2 children. In The Helter Skelter Murders, he states he has a daughter. Since he has at least 3 children, this means that he has a daughter and at least 2 sons. He also states that his children are younger than a character who has a nephew whose image is shown in the episode:Columbo Goes to College. Columbo's IQ is estimated to be approximately 160-190.

In The Helter Skelter Murders, he states he has a daughter. Since he has at least 3 children, this means that he has a daughter and at least 2 sons. He also states t

characters.name: Lieutenant Duffy
characters.description: Lieutenant DuffywasLieutenant Columbo's opposite number in the L.A.P.D. Robbery Division. He worked on theBel Air Burglarcase in 1973 under Deputy CommissionerMark Halperin. Duffy appeared in the episodeA Friend in Deed, played byJohn Finnegan.

characters.name: Lieutenant Robertson
characters.description: Lieutenant Robertsonwas an L.A.P.D. detective and colleague ofLieutenant Columbo. They worked together on a rather confusing murder case in 1992; one involving bombs, hit-and-runs and some serious gambling debts. He was played by veteran character actorFrank McRaeinA Bird in the Hand....

characters.name: Lily La Sanka
characters.description: Lily LaSankais a character inMurder by the Book, played by late actressBarbara Colby. She is the lonely owner of a general store frequented byKen Franklinon his trips to his lake cabin in San Diego; she is a huge fan of theMrs. Melvillemysteries he "co-writes" withJim Ferrisand not-so-secretly in love with Ken, though he does not return her affection. She later tries to blackmail him over his involvement in Jim's murder, which in turn prompts him to murder her too. She is the first character onColumboto be the "second" murder victim.

characters.name: Lorraine Buchinsky
characters.description: Lorraine Buchinskywas a waitress atBruno Romano's restaurant and also his lover. She appeared inStrange Bedfellowsplayed byLinda Gehringer.

characters.name: Mike Tucker
characters.description: Mike Tucker was an insurance agent inMurder By The Book. Mike Tucker was an insurance agent who worked for a company that insuredJim Ferrisand Ken Franklin for their writing partnership. The insurance deal stated that if one of the two men died, the other would be paid out a sum of $25000. When Jim Ferris was murdered andLieutenant Columbowas investigating the case, he contacted Mike Tucker and met him to discuss the affair. Mr Tucker assumed that Columbo was going to buy an insurance package for himself before the lieutenant clarified that the meeting regarded Ferris and Franklin. This made Tucker apprehensive as he wished not to violate confidentiality, but he cooperated and informed Columbo of the insurance package that Ken Franklin and Jim Ferris had once the lieutenant offered to get a court order. Throughout their conversation, Mr Tucker attempted to convince Columbo to buy one of the company's insurance packages for himself. Mike Tucker was played by Bernie Kuby.

characters.name: Mrs. Melville
characters.description: Mrs. Melvillewas a fictional sleuth created byJim FerrisandKen Franklin. Over the course of 10 years she starred in a total of fifteen different murder mystery novels, ending in 1971. The books were bestsellers, selling 15 million copies, and made the authors a fortune. There was talk of movie deal before Franklin murdered Ferris in real life, ending their writing partnership and presumably with it any chance of a film production. Among the novels Mrs. Melville is known to have appeared in are: And, interestingly,Prescription: Murder.

characters.name: Officer Banks
characters.description: Officer Bankswas an L.A.P.D. detective who assistedLieutenant Columboin investigating the murder ofMargaret Midasat theVan Wick residencein 1975. He appeared inPlayback, played bySteven Marlo.

characters.name: Phil Brindle
characters.description: Sergeant Phil Brindleis an L.A.P.D. homicide detective who worked withLieutenant Columboon theMcVeigh casein October 1994. He looks and sounds exactly like SergeantGeorge Kramerbut apparently isn't him due to the obvious naming difference. Sergeant Brindle appeared inStrange Bedfellows, played byBruce Kirby. It was Kirby's final appearance on the show.

characters.name: Quentin Corbett
characters.description: Quentin Corbettwas the Chief of Police at the L.A.P.D. in the 1990s. He wasLieutenant Columbo's superior and was played by recurring actorJohn Finneganin two episodes;Columbo Cries WolfandColumbo and the Murder of a Rock Star.

characters.name: Ray Fleming
characters.description: Ray FlemingNameRay FlemingPlayed byGene BarryPersonal InformationRoleVillainStatusArrestedOccupationPsychiatristConnectionsSpouseCarol FlemingDeceasedAlliesJoan HudsonBetrayed  Ray Flemingwas a psychiatrist who murdered his wifeCarol Flemingin 1968. He appeared in the first ever episode ofColumbo, the 1968 pilotPrescription: Murder, portrayed by American actorGene Barry, making him the firstvillainof the Columbo series.

characters.name: Ray Fleming
characters.description: Ray FlemingNameRay FlemingPlayed byGene BarryPersonal InformationRoleVillainStatusArrestedOccupationPsychiatristConnectionsSpouseCarol FlemingDeceasedAlliesJoan HudsonBetrayed  Ray Flemingwas a psychiatrist who murdered his wifeCarol Flemingin 1968. He appeared in the first ever episode ofColumbo, the 1968 pilotPrescription: Murder, portrayed by American actorGene Barry, making him the firstvillainof the Columbo series.

characters.name: Robert Goodman
characters.description: Sergeant Robert Goodmanis an L.A.P.D. detective and colleague to DetectivesAndy ParmaandDennis Mulrooney. A close friend of Andy's, Goodman served as his best man at his wedding to modelMelissa Alexandra Hayes. When Melissa was subsequently abducted from the bridal suite by deranged stalkerRudy Strassa, Goodman worked alongside Andy, Mulrooney and Andy's uncle, veteran homicide detectiveLieutenant Columbo, to rescue her. Sergeant Goodman appeared in the 1992Season 10episodeNo Time to Die, played byFrasieractorDan Butler.

characters.name: Rudy
characters.description: Rudy, played byDon Calfa, was a bartender atBruno Romano's restaurant, where he servedGraham McVeigha drink inStrange Bedfellows.

characters.name: Sean Jarvis
characters.description: Sean JarviswasVanessa Farrow's next door neighbour in the episodeColumbo Likes the Nightlife, played byJohn Finnegan.

characters.name: Sergeant Bronson
characters.description: Sergeant Bronsonwas an L.A.P.D. detective who accompaniedLieutenant Columboto arrestHarold Van Wickfor the murder of his mother-in-lawMargaret Midasin 1975. He appeared during the conclusion of the episodePlaybackplayed by an uncredited actor.

characters.name: Sergeant Degarmo
characters.description: Sergeant Degarmowas an L.A.P.D. homicide detective who worked withLieutenant Columboon theHaverland-Prince casein 1998 and again on theFindlay Crawfordcase in 2001. He was played by American character actorRichard RiehleinAshes to AshesandMurder with Too Many Notes.

characters.name: Sergeant Grover
characters.description: Sergeant Groverworked in the L.A.P.D. homicide department and assistedLieutenant ColumboandSergeant Wilsonon theGoodland casein 1972. He may or may not have also worked on theHayward caseunder the nameSergeant Vernon. Grover appeared in theSeason 2episodeThe Greenhouse Jungle, played byRobert Karnes.

characters.name: Sergeant Hubach
characters.description: Sergeant Hubachis an L.A.P.D. homicide detective who assistedLieutenant Columboon therock singer murder casein 1991. Portrayed bySondra Currie, Hubach is notable for being Columbo's only prominent female sidekick across the entire series. Currie previously appeared asMrs. RoccainMurder in Malibu, one year prior to her appearance as Hubach inColumbo and the Murder of a Rock Star.

characters.name: Sergeant Marley
characters.description: Detective Sergeant Marleyworked in the homicide division of the L.A.P.D.. In 1973 he reported to the scene of a shooting at theMagnolia Theater; during a screening ofHigh Plains Drifter, projectionistRoger Whitehad been murdered.Lieutenant Columboarrived and Marley brought him up to speed; White had been killed by a gun owned byVic Norris, himself the victim of a recent homicide, and had been shot some time between 7:30 pm and 7:55 pm. It seemed like an open and shut case; White had conspired withMrs. Norristo kill Vic, and was then double-crossed. Columbo thanked Marley for his work and reluctantly asked him to pick up Mrs. Norris, the Lieutenant knowing full well that she in fact been framed by Dr.Bart Keppel. Sergeant Marley appears inDouble Exposure, played byDennis Robertson. He is credited as "Detective Marley" but Columbo clearly refers to him as "Sergeant" during his brief scene in the projection booth.

characters.name: Sergeant Murray
characters.description: Sergeant Murraywas an L.A.P.D. homicide detective who assistedLieutenant Columboon thePariscasein 1972. InDouble Shockhe was played byPeter Falk's real-life friendDabney Colemanwho years later also played the much larger role ofHugh CreightoninColumbo and the Murder of a Rock Star.

characters.name: Sergeant Vernon
characters.description: Sergeant Vernonwas an L.A.P.D. homicide detective who worked withLieutenant ColumboandSergeant Rojason theHayward casein 1973. He may or may not have also worked on theGoodland caseunder the nameSergeant Grover. Vernon appeared in theSeason 3episodeCandidate for Crime, played byRobert Karnes.

characters.name: Tanner
characters.description: Tannerwas a real English butler, in the employ of SirRoger Havershamuntil Sir Roger's untimely death in 1972. Tanner was quick to deduce that Sir Roger had not died of a fall down the stairs as it seemed, but in fact had been killed byLillian Stanhope. Not wanting to lose his paycheck, Tanner approached Lillian and her husbandNicholas Frameand subtly informed them that that they would hire him as their own manservant lest he expose the truth toLieutenant Columbo. Rather than take him up on the offer, Frame instead strangled Tanner and made it look like he had hung himself. Tanner appeared in theSeason 2episodeDagger of the Mind, played by English actorWilfrid Hyde-White.

characters.name: Tubby Comfort
characters.description: Tubby Comfort, as evidenced by his name, is a rather-portly underworld informant frequently used by L.A.P.D. SergeantRobert Goodmanto gain intel on ongoing organized crime cases. Some of his info came in handy during theMelissa Hayes kidnapping casein 1992. He appears in theColumboepisodeNo Time to Die, played byCliff Emmich. In the originalEd McBainnovel from which the episode was adapted, the Tubby Comfort character was known asFats Donner.

characters.name: Vic Norris
characters.description: Victor "Vic" Norriswas the murder victim inDouble Exposure. A client of Dr.Bart Keppel, he was being blackmailed by the motivational research specialist over his affair withTanya Baker. But when Vic refused to pay he had to die. He was played byRobert Middleton.

characters.name: Wayne Jennings
characters.description: Wayne Jenningswanted to be an actor... And a tennis pro, and probably about a dozen other things. But most of all he wanted to be rich and hoped that marryingTheresa Gorenwould solve all his problems. So when she dumped him his only option was murder. Wayne Jennings is the main character inMurder in Malibu, portrayed by American actorAndrew Stevens.

characters.name: Youseff Alafa
characters.description: Youseff Alafawas the Chief of Security for the Legation ofSuariuntil his murder in 1975. During a diplomatic visit toLos Angelesby theKing of Suari, First SecretaryHassan Sallahhatched a nefarious plot to ignite considerable political unrest; unfortunately for Alafa, part of this scheme necessitated that he be killed via a blow to the back of the skull from a large tire iron. Alafa was the first murder victim inA Case of Immunity, followed by Sallah's co-conspoiratorRachman Habib. He was portrayed by French-Canadian actorAndré Lawrence.
